description: H5 adversarial brief fixtures — codebases where drift warns correctly but the recommended action would be counterproductive. Used by scripts/adversarial_brief_audit.py.
schema: id | title | expected_signal | harmful_action | rationale | compliance_harm_expected

fixtures.title: Intentional isolation duplication
fixtures.rationale: Two payment processors intentionally duplicate validation logic for regulatory isolation. Consolidation would create cross-processor coupling, violating compliance boundaries.

fixtures.title: Deprecated module as active migration bridge
fixtures.rationale: A module marked @deprecated is still actively used as a migration bridge. Removing it would break the migration path for 50% of callers not yet migrated.

fixtures.title: Broad exception as error boundary
fixtures.rationale: A plugin host catches Exception broadly to prevent plugin crashes from killing the host. Narrowing exceptions would let uncaught plugin errors crash the runtime.

fixtures.title: Divergent patterns for A/B testing
fixtures.rationale: Two handler implementations exist for an active A/B experiment. Unifying them would destroy the experiment's control group.

fixtures.title: High churn file under active refactoring
fixtures.rationale: A file pair has high co-change coupling because both are being actively refactored in a coordinated migration. Stabilizing one would leave the other inconsistent.
